title: New Year's Day
body: January 1 is New Year's Day, which makes it an easy day for fresh starts, family check-ins, and pretending this is definitely the year everyone will keep their resolutions.
notes: Birthday-theme supplemental starter; verify source/date during later data hardening.

title: National Spaghetti Day
body: January 4 is often celebrated as National Spaghetti Day, a perfect excuse for a big bowl of pasta and the kind of sauce discussion that somehow becomes a family debate.
notes: Birthday-theme supplemental starter; verify source/date during later data hardening.

title: National Popcorn Day
body: January 19 is commonly marked as National Popcorn Day, which is useful because popcorn works for movies, games, gossip, and pretending a snack counts as dinner.
notes: Birthday-theme supplemental starter; verify source/date during later data hardening.

title: Groundhog Day
body: February 2 is Groundhog Day, when a small animal gets a surprising amount of weather responsibility and the rest of us look for signs of spring.
notes: Birthday-theme supplemental starter; verify source/date during later data hardening.

title: Valentine's Day
body: February 14 is Valentine's Day, a built-in excuse for chocolate, flowers, cards, and reminding people that the family group text counts as a love language.
notes: Birthday-theme supplemental starter; verify source/date during later data hardening.

title: National Strawberry Day
body: February 27 is often celebrated as National Strawberry Day, which makes chocolate-covered strawberries feel less like a treat and more like a calendar obligation.
notes: Birthday-theme supplemental starter; verify source/date during later data hardening.

title: Pi Day
body: March 14 is Pi Day, which means math people get their moment and everyone else can quietly focus on the pie part.
notes: Birthday-theme supplemental starter; verify source/date during later data hardening.

title: St. Patrick's Day
body: March 17 is St. Patrick's Day, a day for Irish pride, green everything, and at least one person saying they are Irish for the day.
notes: Birthday-theme supplemental starter; verify source/date during later data hardening.

title: National Puppy Day
body: March 23 is commonly recognized as National Puppy Day, which is a very efficient way for the internet to become mostly paws, ears, and people saying they are not getting another dog.
notes: Birthday-theme supplemental starter; verify source/date during later data hardening.

title: April Fools' Day
body: April 1 is April Fools' Day, when the internet becomes suspicious, family jokes become dangerous, and nobody should trust a headline before coffee.
notes: Birthday-theme supplemental starter; verify source/date during later data hardening.

title: National Deep Dish Pizza Day
body: April 5 is often listed as National Deep Dish Pizza Day, which is either pizza or a casserole depending on who in the family you ask.
notes: Birthday-theme supplemental starter; verify source/date during later data hardening.

title: National Prime Rib Day
body: April 27 is often celebrated as National Prime Rib Day, a holiday that sounds like it was invented by someone who knew exactly what they wanted for dinner.
notes: Birthday-theme supplemental starter; verify source/date during later data hardening.

title: The Beatles on Ed Sullivan
body: The Beatles' first appearance on The Ed Sullivan Show in February 1964 became one of the most famous music moments on American television. It is still shorthand for the moment Beatlemania became impossible to ignore.
notes: Birthday-theme supplemental starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Aretha Franklin Birthday
body: Aretha Franklin was born on March 25, 1942, and became known as the Queen of Soul. Her voice made even familiar songs feel like special occasions.
notes: Birthday-theme supplemental starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Bob Dylan Birthday
body: Bob Dylan was born on May 24, 1941, and changed what people thought a popular song could say. His work made lyrics feel capable of carrying poetry, jokes, protest, and mystery all at once.
notes: Birthday-theme supplemental starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Fenway Park Opens
body: Fenway Park opened in April 1912 and became one of Boston's most recognizable places. Even people who do not follow baseball know the Green Monster has a personality of its own.
notes: Birthday-theme supplemental starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Boston Marathon Week
body: The Boston Marathon is a spring tradition that turns the city into a long ribbon of runners, volunteers, cowbells, and people pretending they are not crying at the finish line.
notes: Birthday-theme supplemental starter; marathon date varies by year, so this uses a week-of spring anchor.

title: Irish Heritage Month
body: March is Irish-American Heritage Month in the United States, which makes it a natural time for Irish history, family stories, and a little extra green on the calendar.
notes: Birthday-theme supplemental month-wide starter.

title: Betty White Birthday
body: Betty White was born on January 17, 1922, and became one of television's most beloved comic performers. Her career made multiple generations feel like they had known her forever.
notes: Birthday-theme supplemental starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Dolly Parton Birthday
body: Dolly Parton was born on January 19, 1946, and built a career around music, humor, generosity, and being completely herself. She is one of those celebrities almost everyone seems happy to root for.
notes: Birthday-theme supplemental starter; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.
